example.catalog.bulk.delete.noneFiltered: Keine Produkte entsprechen den aktuellen Filtern.
example.catalog.bulk.delete.noneSelected: Wählen Sie mindestens ein Produkt zum Löschen aus.

example.mutationLifecycle.description: Validation page for mutation guards, sync event subscribers, client-side event filtering, and command interceptors.
example.mutationLifecycle.hintHeading: What should be visible and how it should work
example.mutationLifecycle.m1.description: Create a todo via the CRUD pipeline to verify the mutation guard registry evaluates the `example.todo-limit` guard on POST.
example.mutationLifecycle.m1.hint1: 1. Guard `example.todo-limit` targets `example.todo` on `create` operations with priority 50.
example.mutationLifecycle.m1.hint2: 2. Guard validates `organizationId` presence — creation fails with 422 if missing.
example.mutationLifecycle.m1.hint3: 3. Multiple guards run by priority order; first rejection stops the pipeline.
example.mutationLifecycle.m1.hint4: 4. Legacy `crudMutationGuardService` is automatically bridged as a guard with priority 0.
example.mutationLifecycle.m1.title: Phase m1 — Mutation Guard Registry
example.mutationLifecycle.m2.createFaile: Could not create todo for sync subscriber probe.
example.mutationLifecycle.m2.description: Run a multi-step probe: create a todo (verify auto-default-priority), mark as done then try to revert (verify prevent-uncomplete blocks with 422), and delete (verify audit-delete fires).
example.mutationLifecycle.m2.hint1: 1. `auto-default-priority`: Injects `priority: 'normal'` when creating a todo without explicit priority (sync before-create).
example.mutationLifecycle.m2.hint2: 2. `prevent-uncomplete`: Blocks reverting completed todos to pending with 422 (sync before-update).
example.mutationLifecycle.m2.hint3: 3. `audit-delete`: Logs deletion audit trail to server console (sync after-delete, non-blocking).
example.mutationLifecycle.m2.hint4: 4. Sync subscribers run inside the CRUD pipeline, before/after the actual mutation — unlike async subscribers which run post-commit.
example.mutationLifecycle.m2.partial: One or more sync subscriber probes failed. Review rows below.
example.mutationLifecycle.m2.probe.audit: Todo deleted (200). Sync after-event example.todo.deleted fired — audit-delete subscriber logged to server console.
example.mutationLifecycle.m2.probe.defau: Todo created (201). Sync before-event example.todo.creating fired — auto-default-priority subscriber injects priority if absent.
example.mutationLifecycle.m2.probe.preve: Failed to mark todo as done: status {value}.
Revert blocked (422). Sync before-event example.todo.updating fired — prevent-uncomplete subscriber rejected the operation.
example.mutationLifecycle.m2.probe.skipp: Skipped — no todo was created in probe 1.
example.mutationLifecycle.m2.title: Phase m2 — Sync Event Subscribers
example.mutationLifecycle.m3.description: Widget injection event handlers can now declare an operation filter to skip events for specific CRUD operations.
example.mutationLifecycle.m3.hint1: 1. Widgets can declare `eventHandlers.filter.operations` to skip specific CRUD operations (e.g., only fire on `update`).
example.mutationLifecycle.m3.hint2: 2. CrudForm now passes `operation` ('create' or 'update') in the injection context.
example.mutationLifecycle.m3.hint3: 3. Widgets without a filter continue to fire for all operations (backward compatible).
example.mutationLifecycle.m3.note: The filter is a type-level extension. See Phase G page for CrudForm injection demo.
example.mutationLifecycle.m3.title: Phase m3 — Client-Side Event Filtering
example.mutationLifecycle.m4.description: The `example.audit-logging` interceptor wraps all `customers.*` command bus operations with timing metadata.
example.mutationLifecycle.m4.hint3: 3. `afterExecute` reads metadata and logs: `[example:audit] Command {id} completed in {ms}ms`.
example.mutationLifecycle.m4.hint4: 4. Pattern matching supports `*` (all), exact ID, and `prefix.*` (namespace wildcard).
example.mutationLifecycle.m4.hint5: 5. Interceptors run on both `execute()` and `undo()` code paths.
example.mutationLifecycle.m4.note: Create or edit a customer, then check server console for the audit log entry.
example.mutationLifecycle.m4.title: Phase m4 — Command Interceptors
example.mutationLifecycle.title: UMES Phase M — Mutation Lifecycle
example.notifications.umesActionable.bod: Open the UMES next phases page to verify reactive notification handlers.
example.notifications.umesActionable.tit: Action required in UMES next phases
example.payments.description: Test payment gateway integrations with mock or real providers
example.payments.error.forbidden: You do not have permission to create payment sessions. Check your role permissions.
example.payments.error.providerNotFound: Payment provider not found. Make sure the gateway adapter is registered.
example.payments.error.title: Error
example.payments.setup.mockDesc: The mock gateway works out of the box. Click "Pay with Mock Gateway" below to test the full payment lifecycle: create session, capture, refund, and cancel.
example.payments.setup.showWebhookGuide: Show me how to configure webhook
example.payments.setup.step1: Create a Stripe account at stripe.com and get your API keys from the Stripe Dashboard.
example.payments.setup.step2: Go to Settings > Integrations in the admin panel. Find "Stripe" and enter your Publishable Key, Secret Key, and Webhook Signing Secret.
example.payments.setup.step3: Go to Settings > Sales > Payment Methods. Create a new payment method with Provider Key set to "stripe".
example.payments.setup.step4: For webhooks, configure Stripe to send events to: {YOUR_APP_URL}/api/payment_gateways/webhook/stripe
example.payments.setup.step5: Come back here and click "Pay with Stripe" to test.
example.payments.setup.title: How to Configure Payment Gateways
example.payments.setup.webhookSummary: Stripe credentials are configured in Integrations, and a webhook endpoint is recommended so payment lifecycle updates stay synchronized.
example.payments.stripe.form.authorized: Payment authorized successfully. Use Capture to settle the funds.
example.payments.stripe.form.description: Enter a test card, confirm the payment, then use Capture if the payment becomes authorized.
example.payments.stripe.form.notReady: Payment form is not ready yet.
example.payments.stripe.form.testCard: Use Stripe test card `4242 4242 4242 4242`, any future date, any CVC.
example.payments.stripe.form.title: Complete Stripe payment
example.payments.stripeUnavailable: Stripe unavailable — enable the integration and save credentials in Integrations
example.payments.success.title: Success
example.payments.title: Payment Gateway Demo

example.umes.extensions.form.title: Titel
example.umes.extensions.hintHeading: What should be visible and how it should work
example.umes.extensions.phaseE.descripti: Run the full probe suite: metadata merge, wildcard route matching, query revalidation, timeout fail-closed, and crash fail-closed.
example.umes.extensions.phaseE.hint1: 1. `default` probe: must return `_example.interceptor` metadata in `/api/example/todos` response.
example.umes.extensions.phaseE.hint2: 2. `wildcard` probe: must return `_example.wildcardProbe=true` for wildcard route interceptor.
example.umes.extensions.phaseE.hint3: 3. `bad-query` probe: must fail with HTTP `400` (route schema revalidation after interceptor rewrite).
example.umes.extensions.phaseE.hint4: 4. `timeout` probe: must fail closed with HTTP `504`.
example.umes.extensions.phaseE.hint5: 5. `crash` probe: must fail closed with HTTP `500`.
example.umes.extensions.phaseE.hint6: Note: red network entries for probes 3-5 are expected and indicate correct fail-closed behavior.
example.umes.extensions.phaseE.missing: Eine oder mehrere Interceptor-Prüfungen fehlgeschlagen. Zeilen unten überprüfen.
example.umes.extensions.phaseE.title: Phase E — API-Interceptoren
example.umes.extensions.phaseF.descripti: This table exposes `replacementHandle` and known component handles. Use it to validate replacement registration and then verify injected columns/actions on Customers list.
example.umes.extensions.phaseF.expect: Expect: injected column, row action, filters, and bulk action.
example.umes.extensions.phaseF.hint1: 1. On `/backend/customers/people` table: column `Example priority` should be visible.
example.umes.extensions.phaseF.hint2: 2. In filters drawer: select filter `Priority` should be visible.
example.umes.extensions.phaseF.hint3: 3. In row actions menu: action `Open customer` should be visible.
example.umes.extensions.phaseF.hint4: 4. After selecting rows: bulk action `Set normal priority` should update priorities via API.
example.umes.extensions.phaseF.title: Phase F — DataTable-Erweiterungen
example.umes.extensions.phaseG.descripti: This harness keeps the injected widget active on `crud-form:example.todo`. Submit once to confirm the field/event pipeline executes.
example.umes.extensions.phaseG.hint1: 1. Injected widget card `Example Injection Widget` should be visible above form fields.
example.umes.extensions.phaseG.hint2: 2. Saving valid form should update `submitResult` below the form.
example.umes.extensions.phaseG.hint3: 3. In customer detail form (`/backend/customers/people/:id`), injected `_example.priority` field should persist via onSave handler.
example.umes.extensions.phaseG.title: Phase G — CrudForm-Feldeinspeisung
example.umes.extensions.phaseH.descripti: Active replacement handles in this area: page, DataTable, CrudForm, and the `ui.detail:NotesSection` wrapper declared in `example/widgets/components.ts`.
example.umes.extensions.phaseH.hint2: 2. Handles list table should expose `data-table:example.umes.extensions` replacement handle.
example.umes.extensions.phaseH.hint4: 4. Customer detail notes section should render wrapped border from `ExampleNotesSectionWrapper`.
example.umes.extensions.phaseH.title: Phase H — Komponentenersetzung
example.umes.extensions.table.label: Bezeichnung
example.umes.extensions.table.title: Ersetzungs-Handles
example.umes.extensions.title: UMES Phasen E-H Erweiterungen

example.umes.handlers.guide.description: Für jedes bedienbare Element ist das erwartete Ergebnis unten beschrieben.
example.umes.handlers.guide.expect.autoR: Führt Server-Event + App-Event + Enricher-Probe aus, um Bereitschaftszeilen nach Möglichkeit auf grün zu setzen.
example.umes.handlers.guide.expect.cance: Versucht Navigation zu /backend/blocked; Widget onBeforeNavigate kann blockieren.
example.umes.handlers.guide.expect.field: Nimmt vor dem Senden am transformFormData-Trimmen teil.
Eingabe löst die Widget-Field-Change-Pipeline aus; Werte mit TEST sollten eine Warnung zeigen.
example.umes.handlers.guide.expect.loadB: Lädt Formulardaten, die durch die Widget-onBeforeSave-Regel blockiert werden sollen ([block]-Marker).
example.umes.handlers.guide.expect.loadT: Lädt Daten, die eine Dialogbestätigung und anschließende transformFormData-Transformation auslösen.
example.umes.handlers.guide.expect.mockA: Sendet lokales DOM-App-Event und aktualisiert appEventResult/Widget-App-Event-Status.
example.umes.handlers.guide.expect.navig: Sollte auf der aktuellen Seite bleiben, mit Widget-Navigationsergebnis ok=false.
example.umes.handlers.guide.expect.perso: Wenn gesetzt, prüft die Probe genau diesen Kunden/Person-Datensatz.
example.umes.handlers.guide.expect.probe: Wenn nicht leer, wird vor dem Kunden-API-Request ein Probe-Todo erstellt.
example.umes.handlers.guide.expect.runPr: Ruft die Kunden-API auf und speichert _example-Felder + _meta.enrichedBy.
example.umes.handlers.guide.expect.save: Speichert Submit-Payload in submitResult und führt Transform-/Validierungs-Handler aus.
example.umes.handlers.guide.expect.serve: Erstellt Todo per API und sollte example.todo.created über die Event-Bridge senden.
example.umes.handlers.guide.title: Erwartetes Verhalten von Aktionen
example.umes.handlers.page.description: Mit dieser Seite können UMES-Funktionen aus Phase A-D getestet werden, inklusive Menü-Injection, Handlern und Enrichern.

example.umes.integrations.badges.descrip: Four status badge renderers with fixed loaders demonstrating healthy, warning, error, and unknown states.
example.umes.integrations.badges.hint1: 1. Four badges should render with colored dots (green, yellow, red, gray).
example.umes.integrations.badges.hint2: 2. Hovering over badges with tooltips should show the tooltip text.
example.umes.integrations.badges.hint3: 3. Clicking `Cycle statuses` should rotate all badge statuses forward.
example.umes.integrations.badges.title: L.2 Status Badge Injection
example.umes.integrations.description: Validation page for Phase L integration extensions: multi-step wizard, status badges, external ID mapping, and integration registry.
example.umes.integrations.externalIds.de: ExternalIdsWidget renders mock integration mappings for Shopify and Stripe.
example.umes.integrations.externalIds.hi: 1. Two integration rows should display: Shopify (synced, green dot) and Stripe (pending, yellow dot).
2. Shopify row should show an external link icon.
3. Each row shows the external ID in a monospace code badge.
example.umes.integrations.hintHeading: What should be visible and how it should work
example.umes.integrations.registry.descr: Demonstrates registerIntegration(), getAllIntegrations(), and getIntegrationTitle() from the shared registry.
example.umes.integrations.registry.hint1: 1. Two integrations should be registered on mount (sync_shopify, gateway_stripe).
example.umes.integrations.registry.title: L.4 Integration Registry
example.umes.integrations.title: UMES Phase L — Integration Extensions
example.umes.integrations.wizard.descrip: Complete the 3-step wizard to validate step navigation, per-step validation, and onComplete callback.
example.umes.integrations.wizard.hint1: 1. Step indicator should show 3 numbered circles with connecting lines.
example.umes.integrations.wizard.hint2: 2. Step 1 requires both `apiKey` and `apiSecret` — leaving them empty should show validation error.
example.umes.integrations.wizard.hint3: 3. Completing all steps should display the accumulated wizard data below.
example.umes.integrations.wizard.step1.d: Enter API credentials for the external system.
example.umes.integrations.wizard.title: L.1 Multi-Step Wizard Widget
example.umes.integrations.wizard.validat: Both API key and API secret are required.
example.umes.next.notifications.descript: Emit an actionable notification and verify that registered client handlers react without opening the notifications panel.
example.umes.next.notifications.title: Reactive notification handlers
example.umes.next.page.description: Validation page for SPEC-042 (multi-id query) and SPEC-043 (reactive notification handlers).
example.umes.next.page.title: UMES Next Phases
example.umes.next.probe.description: Use ids=<uuid1,uuid2> against customers API and verify response enrichers still extend the filtered response.
example.umes.next.probe.idsRequired: Provide at least one customer UUID.
example.umes.next.probe.title: Multi-ID enricher probe
example.umes.next.progress.demoJobDescri: SSE demo job for progress bar updates.
example.umes.next.progress.description: Create and stream a synthetic progress job to verify the top progress bar updates via SSE.
example.umes.next.progress.title: Progress SSE simulator
example.umes.queryExtensions.description: Validation page for Phase N query engine extensions: enricher opt-in, surface-aware registry, sync query events, and scope guard re-application.
example.umes.queryExtensions.enrichers.d: Registers three enrichers (API-only, query-enabled for all engines, basic-only) and probes `getEnrichersForEntity` with different surface selectors.
example.umes.queryExtensions.enrichers.h: 1. No selector / `api-response` surface: returns all 3 enrichers (backward compatible).
2. `query-engine` surface (no engine filter): returns 2 enrichers (query-enabled + basic-only).
3. `query-engine` + `basic` engine: returns 2 enrichers (query-enabled defaults to both, basic-only matches).
4. `query-engine` + `hybrid` engine: returns 1 enricher (only query-enabled, basic-only excluded).
example.umes.queryExtensions.entityIds.d: Validates `entityIdToEventEntity` which converts query engine format (`module:entity`) to event format (`module.entity`).
example.umes.queryExtensions.hintHeading: What should be visible and how it should work
example.umes.queryExtensions.scopeGuards: Demonstrates that `reapplyScopeGuards` restores tenant/org constraints after subscriber modifications while preserving other query options.
1. Tampered tenantId/organizationId should be restored to original values.
2. Non-scope fields (filters, withDeleted) should remain unchanged.
3. This ensures subscribers cannot bypass multi-tenant isolation.
example.umes.queryExtensions.subscribers: Registers three sync subscribers (querying, queried, wildcard) and probes `collectQuerySubscribers` with different event IDs.
example.umes.queryExtensions.title: UMES Phase N — Query Engine Extensibility
